title: Bridge Essay: The role of functionality in the body model for self-attribution.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Birth/Body** (Phase 1) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Birth/Body to mechanistic neuroscience through The, role, of, functionality, in.

**The role of functionality in the body model for self-attribution.** — Neurosci Res. PMID: 26602981. DOI: 10.1016/j.neures.2015.11.001. Bodily self-attribution, the feeling that abody (or parts of it) is owned by me, is afundamental component of one'sself. Previous studies have suggested that, in addition to anecessary multi-sensory stimulation, the sense of body ownership is determined by the body model, arepresentation of our body in the brain. It is however unclear what features constitute the body representation. To examine this issue, we first briefly review results on embodiment of artificial limbs, whole bodies and v
